text: Ten honey samples, produced by *Apis mellifera* L. in Roraima State, Brazil, were analyzed for color, total phenolic and flavonoid contents, and their antioxidant activities. The phenolic content was estimated using the Folin-Ciocalteu reagent and was in the range of 250 to 548 mg gallic acid kg⁻¹ honey. The flavonoid content was estimated using two distinct spectrophotometric methods namely, aluminum chloride for total flavones/flavonols and 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine for flavonones/dihydroflavonols. The amounts were in the range of 9 to 48.6 mg quercetin kg⁻¹ honey and 1805 to 2606 mg pinocembrin kg⁻¹ honey, respectively. The DPPH radical scavenging activity gave the IC₅₀ values ranging from 3.17 to 8.79 mg/mL. By statistical analysis, it was inferred that honey with higher color intensity (darker honeys) exhibited more phenolic contents, flavones and flavonols, which showed higher antioxidant activity. The flavonone and dihydroflavonol content was very high but did not give any significant correlation to the parameters estimated in this work.
